skill.name: image-to-video
skill.description: Still-to-video conversion guide: model selection, motion prompting, and camera movement. Covers Wan 2.5 i2v, Seedance, Fabric, Grok Video with when to use each. Use for: animating images, creating video from stills, adding motion, product animations. Triggers: image to video, i2v, animate image, still to video, add motion to image, image animation, photo to video, animate still, wan i2v, image2video, bring image to life, animate photo, motion from image
skill.summary: Guide for converting still images to videos using AI models with motion prompting and camera movement techniques
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. This is a documentation skill that provides guidance on AI video generation models. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are code examples in Markdown blocks showing how to use the external inference.sh CLI tool. The 'network' findings are legitimate documentation links. No executable code, no security risks.

content.user_title: Animate Still Images into Videos with AI
content.value_statement: Transform static images into animated videos using AI models. This guide provides model selection, motion prompting techniques, and camera movement strategies for professional video generation from still images.
actual_capabilities: Provides guidance on selecting the right AI model for different image-to-video scenarios
Teaches motion prompting techniques for natural, realistic animations
Explains camera movement patterns for professional video results
Includes workflow examples for complete still-to-video pipelines
Covers duration guidelines and quality optimization strategies
Documents common mistakes and their fixes for video generation
limitations: This skill provides guidance only and requires external CLI tools to execute
Generated video quality depends on the source image resolution
Longer video durations may result in quality degradation
Complex action scenes may not render well with current models

use_cases.title: Product Marketing Animation
use_cases.description: E-commerce teams can animate product images to create engaging social media content and showcase items from multiple angles

use_cases.title: Cinematic Content Creation
use_cases.description: Video creators can convert landscape photography into cinematic clips with subtle motion for documentaries or background footage

use_cases.title: Portrait Animation
use_cases.description: Photographers can add subtle life to portrait photos with breathing motion and hair movement for artistic projects

prompt_templates.title: Basic Image Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me animate this image for social media. I want subtle motion that looks natural.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user wants to add motion to a static image

prompt_templates.title: Product Showcase with Camera Movement
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to animate this product image with a 360-degree orbit effect. The camera should rotate around the product smoothly.
prompt_templates.scenario: E-commerce user creating product demonstration video

prompt_templates.title: Landscape Cinematic Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic video from this landscape photo. I want water ripples, clouds drifting, and a slow dolly forward camera movement.
prompt_templates.scenario: Content creator working with landscape photography

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Clip Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to create a 15-second video from this image. Generate multiple 5-second clips and stitch them together with progressive motion.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user requiring longer video duration

output_examples.input: Help me animate this portrait photo
output_examples.output: Based on your portrait, I recommend using Wan 2.5 i2v model for best face fidelity. Use this prompt: 'subtle breathing motion, slight head turn, natural eye blink, hair moving gently, soft ambient lighting shifts'

output_examples.input: I want to animate this fabric product image
output_examples.output: For fabric and cloth, the Fabric 1.0 model is specialized in material physics. Try this prompt: 'fabric flowing and rippling in gentle wind, natural cloth physics, soft movement'

output_examples.input: Create a video from this landscape with water
output_examples.output: Use Wan 2.5 i2v for realistic water motion. Prompt: 'gentle camera pan right, water reflecting moving clouds, trees swaying slightly in breeze, warm golden light, peaceful and slow'

best_practices: Always prefer subtle motion over dramatic action for best quality results
Specify camera movement explicitly to avoid unpredictable motion directions
Keep video clips to 3-5 seconds for optimal quality, stitch multiple clips for longer videos
Start with the highest resolution source image available to ensure video quality
anti_patterns: Avoid requesting complex action scenes like running, jumping, or explosions as current models cannot handle them well
Do not generate clips longer than 10 seconds as quality degrades significantly
Never skip camera movement specifications or you may get random, unnatural motion
Avoid requesting multiple conflicting motion directions in a single prompt

faq.question: What is the difference between Wan 2.5 and Seedance models?
faq.answer: Wan 2.5 i2v produces realistic, photorealistic motion and is best for landscapes and portraits. Seedance models create stylized, artistic animations and work well with illustrated content.

faq.question: How long should my video clips be for best quality?
faq.answer: Keep clips to 3-5 seconds for high quality. For longer videos, generate multiple short clips and stitch them together. Quality degrades significantly at 10+ seconds.

faq.question: Why does my video look distorted or warped?
faq.answer: You likely requested too much motion. The golden rule is subtle over dramatic. Reduce the amount of movement in your prompt and try again.

faq.question: Can I use this skill without installing external tools?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance on using the inference.sh CLI tool which must be installed separately. The skill teaches you how to use the tool effectively.

faq.question: What model should I use for fabric and cloth animation?
faq.answer: Use the Fabric 1.0 model which is specialized in material physics. It handles cloth simulation and flowing materials better than general-purpose models.

faq.question: How do I create a cinemagraph effect?
faq.answer: Generate your image with the motion element clearly defined, then prompt for motion only in that specific element. Keep the video to 2-4 seconds for seamless looping and specify that everything else remains still.
